question: Is the grey L-shaped sofa now positioned on the right side of the room?

Are the black dining chairs arranged around the dining table on the left side?
Is the woven pendant lamp hanging above the dining table on the left side?
Is the round marble coffee table positioned in front of the sofa on the right side?
Is the gold floor lamp standing on the right side of the room?
Is the gallery wall of black-framed pictures mounted on the wall on the right side?
Is the patterned area rug positioned under the seating area on the right side?
Do the repositioned furniture pieces align correctly with the room's perspective lines and vanishing points?
Do the shadows cast by the moved furniture align consistently with the light sources from the windows?
Is the pendant lamp correctly attached to the ceiling on the left side without floating or missing cords?
Are the furniture pieces placed without physically impossible overlaps with walls, windows, or the fireplace?
Are the large windows and the snowy winter forest scene view preserved without alteration?
Does the built-in fireplace and the fire within it remain unchanged in position and appearance?
Are the hardwood flooring, walls, and ceiling preserved without structural distortion or texture loss?
Are the areas where furniture was removed (e.g., original sofa and dining spots) free from ghosting, blurred textures, or leftover artifacts?
Are all moved objects (chairs, sofa, frames) rendered with clean edges and no unnatural distortion or missing parts?
Do the furniture pieces have realistic contact shadows on the floor to prevent a 'floating' appearance?
Are textures (rug pattern, lamp weave, sofa fabric) preserved and sharp in their new locations?
Is the image free from duplicated furniture items (e.g., two sofas or two dining tables)?
